meta.title: The Long Investor — Asset Recommendations Dashboard
meta.subtitle: Elliott Wave analysis from TLI emails (last 30 days) · Assets & collections parsed from email subjects ($TICKER - COLLECTION) · Click any ticker for full TLI summary

assets.summary: TLI issued a BUY ALERT at $94 and states plainly that BABA is a buy right now: price has given a strong pullback, is below the 200 WMA, and RSI is oversold. The Wave 4 ABC correction is near completion, setting up a Wave 5 impulse toward $190+. The lower it dips below the 200 WMA, the higher the margin of safety.

ZETA jumped 5% then extended gains after-hours following CEO comments repositioning the company as an AI infrastructure business, alongside a newly announced Palantir partnership. TLI notes 12 of 14 covering analysts rate the stock a buy, and views the pop as a fresh higher high building on support at the 50-week MA. A break above the $26 range line would confirm the impulse move toward the Wave 3 target of $37.

WYFI swung hard, dropping 10% before rocketing up 15%, with TLI adding near the low at $30.90 where the 0.618 Fib, a wedge-breakout level, and the 1-hour 200MA all lined up as confluence support. TLI added again on a retest of the 0.5 Fib at $34.40, and the stock is now building a small bullish wedge under the 50MA/channel breakout level at $36. A confirmed break of $36 would validate the move toward the Wave 3 target of $58.

TLI reiterates ADUR as a bullish continuation: a consolidated wedge broke out, retested, and held, followed by a Golden Cross, and the stock is now using the 50-day MA as support. TLI frames this as Wave 3 underway toward $31, roughly a further +100% from the current $15 price.

GRRR pulled back in a clean three-wave structure into the confluence support of the 1-hour 200MA and the 0.618 Fib near $17.50, the same zone that has held since the June 9 low. TLI sees the hold as confirmation the stock is basing before resuming higher into Wave 3. A hold around $18 is flagged as a good level to add to the position now.

With moving averages failing to hold this week, TLI is watching standard Fibonacci support instead, expecting a hold in the $74-$77 zone to confirm this pullback is just a subwave 2 within an ongoing bullish impulse. A hold here would form a higher low and give another entry point. TLI remains unconcerned about the setup and expects new highs over the next 5-6 weeks.

SE is in the 'sweet spot', consolidating sideways and oscillating around the 200 WMA after a long, patient wait. TLI says the reversal is starting and the position is beginning to gain, targeting $140 as Wave 3 develops.

As earnings season winds down and rotation into defensives builds, TLI flags PFE as one of the best Safe Haven setups, paying a 7.1% dividend. It is an income-and-capital-preservation accumulation, not a high-beta growth call.

TLI says it is again time to treat Silver as a very attractive entry and a Safe Haven, with price retesting the 0.618 Fib / 200 WMA confluence. It is TLI's preferred precious-metal entry, targeting $45 on the next Wave 3 impulse.

GOOG dipped just below the 2.618 Fib at $351. TLI wants to give price the chance to reclaim this level and continue the wave up. Watch for a bullish confirmation / weekly close back above $351 before entering; the next objective is $453 (3.618 Fib).
AMZN tested the 50 WMA at $231 for support, briefly dipped below and reclaimed it to close the week at $232. TLI's target entry is the 50 WMA; once Wave 4 completes there, Wave 5 targets $290.

AAPL pulled back with the market and got some Friday relief, closing near $283. TLI is watching the 50 WMA (~$260) as the buy zone for the Wave 4 low before a Wave 5 push toward $400. Patient — not chasing.
MSFT made a lower low, so TLI now treats this as a Wave C, with the 0.5 Fib at $344 the focus. A hold there sets up the next impulse toward $386 first and $450+ longer term.
SHOP closed the week above the 0.618 Fib at $113. TLI's focus is holding that level; if it holds, the path opens toward $227 (2.618 Fib) — over 100% from entry.

PLTR completed its victory lap by hitting the long-called $106 level. Price has since dropped fast toward the 0.5 Fib at $106, which TLI flags as the buy zone. A hold there sets up the Wave 4 → Wave 5 entry toward $180.

BIDU dipped into TLI's 'load the boat' zone below $105, then rallied from $100 to $119 on news its chip arm would IPO in Hong Kong. TLI notes a possible head-and-shoulders at the 200 WMA, so the disciplined re-entry is a pullback to ~$105. Downgraded from Buy Now to Trending to Buy after the run.
After a slow 12 months, NVO is 'starting to show its worth right when it's needed', consolidating above the 0.618 Fib and 50 DMA. TLI wants to see price flip the 200 DMA to confirm the next leg.

CRM is within reach of the 0.887 Fib at $142, where TLI wants Wave C to complete. Downside risk is seen as limited, with price expected to stabilise in the Buy Zone between the 0.78 and 0.887 Fib before turning up.

TLI calls NFLX's chart one of the cleanest Elliott Wave examples they've seen, with price holding above the $70 invalidation line and climbing to $77. Holding the 0.5 Fib retracement at $75 sets up the next buy entry, with Wave 5 now targeting $201 longer term.

TLI is re-highlighting REGN as an especially attractive setup, just shy of naming it a Safe Haven. After Wave 1 ran from $476 to $813, the stock pulled back in three waves and is holding the 0.618 Fib as Wave 2 support, backed by strong fundamentals and defensive positioning in a pharma sector decoupled from the broader market. TLI's verdict is an explicit Strong Buy, with Wave 3 now targeting $1166.
The battleground is the 50 WMA at $402, which TSLA is struggling to hold. If $402 is not reclaimed, TLI's next level to watch is the 0.5 Fib around $300 — a level to monitor, not an entry.
Little to add on NIO; TLI's focus is simply holding a higher low and staying above the February lows. Until the structure confirms a bottom, there is no entry signal.
NKE remains within a large descending wedge and reported earnings this week. TLI is watching the 0.78 Fib (~$39) as potential support and waiting on the earnings reaction before re-evaluating.

OSCR is starting to bend — no lower low yet, but under pressure below the green resistance at $30 after news the CEO sold shares. Downgraded from Appreciating to Trending Negatively; TLI wants to see whether support holds.

TLI's read: the market has turned its back on crypto and we are close to capitulation, with sentiment reset. This is a contrarian accumulation stance — no fresh buy alert yet — with the 200 WMA the level to watch for the next entry before a longer-term move toward $180,000. Downgraded from Buy Now to Trending Negatively short-term.

PEP bounced off blue support at $127 up to the 200 WMA near $170 but rejected heavily. TLI tags it 'Safe Haven soon' — a defensive name to watch as rotation into safe havens builds, but not an entry yet.

TLI expects members to rotate into Safe Havens over the coming months and keeps Palladium on watch, but the structure is still corrective. TLI advises waiting for support to confirm before treating it as an entry.

AMD continued to hold above the 1.618 Fib and closed near $521. If it keeps holding, Wave 4 is complete and Wave 5 is underway toward $686. Already appreciating — TLI advises against chasing; the ideal entry was the Wave 4 low.

V is behaving exactly as expected for the start of a Wave 5 — it retraced to the 0.38 Fib just below $300 and bounced. TLI's Wave 5 objective is $411 (2.0 Fib). Late-cycle, so new entries are unfavorable.

TLI's high-conviction Buffett-aligned position is playing out. UNH is in Wave 1 toward $446–$460; once Wave 1 tops, TLI expects a Wave 2 correction to create the next buy opportunity for the larger Wave 3.

DCTH keeps ignoring the market — up +7% on a day the QQQ was down 3% — and is approaching TLI's small Wave 3 target at the 1.618 Fib (~$22). A strong hold since the $10 support add; TLI would wait for a Wave 4 pullback before adding more.

assets.entry: $13.20 (retest of broken resistance / former Wave 1 high)
assets.summary: TLI notes ZVRA broke above the green resistance line and the old Wave 1 high near $13.20, keeping the setup firmly bullish. A retest of that level holding as support would be viewed as a clean buy signal, though for now price is running higher without pulling back. The long-term Wave 3 target has been raised to the 1.618 Fib extension near $22.

MELI dipped into TLI's buy zone below the 0.5 Fib at $1,621 and the 200-week moving average at $1,686 exactly as anticipated, and has since reclaimed the 200 WMA. The stock is now working toward the 50 WMA and the psychological $2,000 level; a breakout above $2,000 would confirm the bull case is back on. A retest of the 200 WMA would offer another low-risk entry.

Ahead of NVDA's expected August 26 earnings, TLI's focus is on the stock holding above the 50-week moving average at $188 and eventually reclaiming the 1 Fib at $228 from below. Longer term, TLI is still watching for the completion of Wave 5, which it views as essential fuel for the broader market's melt-up, with a bull-case target of $266.

TOST was added to the watchlist in early June after dropping into TLI's Wave 2 buy zone between the 0.618 and 0.786 Fib. That call has played out, with price reclaiming the 200 WMA at $27.49, and attention now shifts to a challenge of the 50 WMA at $32.91. The long-term Wave 3 target remains the 1.618 Fib at $83.

JD retraced back to test the March lows around $24.50 and buyers stepped in quickly, a reaction TLI sees as encouraging for a possible double bottom. The next hurdle is confluence resistance near $30 from the 50-week MA, a descending trendline, and psychological round-number resistance. A flip of $30 to support would put JD back in the bull case.

ONDS is still struggling to reclaim the 0.5 Fib at $7.94, a level TLI wants to see retaken sooner rather than later. Beyond that sits tougher confluence resistance between the 200-day MA and 50-day MA, which would need to flip to support before a move toward the bull-case line at $11.70. The stock is still inside the buy zone but carries downside risk to the 0.618 Fib at $6.20.

HIMS briefly touched $39.05, nearly hitting TLI's Wave 3 target at the 1.618 Fib after months of TLI calling for a move back toward $40. TLI is now watching for Wave 3 to reject here and start a typical Wave 4 pullback, ideally down to the confluence of the 1 Fib and 200-day MA near $32, which would offer a strong spot to add before Wave 5. The longer-term Wave 5 target remains $54.

TLI notes SOFI broke above the $18.80 triangle resistance but failed to hold it through Thursday's market weakness, then rebounded strongly this week toward $19. A sustained hold above $18.80 would confirm Wave 3 is underway, with the 1.618 Fib extension at $23.40 as the long-term target.

In a brief tweet repost, TLI (not personally invested) flags a bullish wedge on OPEN holding support at the 0.618 Fib for Wave 2, trading near $4.90. TLI expects the setup to draw attention, with staged upside targets at $10, $20, and ultimately $31.

COIN is consolidating under its 200-week moving average after a three-wave pullback and wedge formation between the 0.618 and 0.786 Fib levels. TLI pegs $185-$190, where the 200 WMA, wedge breakout, and 0.618 Fib converge, as the key buy zone, though a real move up likely needs crypto broadly to reverse first.

TLI calls HYPE's setup clean and bullish after price broke above the $59 Bull Case line and retested the confluence with the 50-day moving average twice. The near-term trigger is a breakout above $75; clearing it opens a path to the 1.618 Fib Wave 3 target of $102, provided price holds above the 50-day MA near $63.

CIFR completed another expanded flat correction, with Wave C pulling back to the 0.618 Fib at $18.81 before premarket strength lifted shares 3% alongside peers IREN and NBIS. TLI views this as a healthy, low-risk entry early in Wave 3, with plenty of upside left toward the $53 target.

NBIS is up premarket near $220, which TLI reads as another healthy retest of support similar to IREN, after a three-wave pullback that reached the 0.38 Fib near $200, a textbook Wave 4 target. The near-term focus is holding above the 50-day MA at $215 and then breaking the 1.618 Fib at $282, which would confirm Wave 5 toward the $411 target.

IREN jumped 5% premarket to $40, and TLI reads the recent pullback as a textbook ABC correction into its Buy Zone, having tested the 0.78 Fib near $39 with buyers stepping back in. The next steps are reclaiming the 50-week MA at $44 as support, then breaking above the $63 Bull Case line to resume the longer-term uptrend.

MU dropped another 5% premarket to a lower low after Samsung's earnings triggered a broad AI-chip selloff, even though Samsung itself posted a large profit jump. TLI notes the 1.618 Fib support has now been lost, sliding from the mid-$1200s toward $930, and expects the decline to extend toward the 50-day MA near $862. TLI treats this as just Wave A of a larger corrective move.
